sectionOrderRules: Hero must state the business type and primary offer, and should mention location when known.
Service summary must cover concrete service categories such as magasinering, varulagring, terminal- eller transportstöd rather than generic business claims.
Facility highlights should appear before the final CTA so visitors understand practical conditions before contacting.
Trust proof must be factual, such as organisation details, references or clear operating information, and must not invent certifications or capacities.
Sections should avoid repeating the same operational benefit in multiple blocks.
Services intro must connect the company's role in the logistics chain to the offered services.
Service list items must be concrete and operational, for example lagring, omlastning, orderhantering, terminaltjänster or distributionsstöd where relevant.
Handling process should explain how goods are received, stored, handled or released at a high level without making unverifiable promises.
If pricing summary is shown, it should present honest pricing structure cues or quote-based information, not fabricated fixed prices.
Facility intro must describe the site or storage environment in practical terms.
Capacity and access should focus on storage types, loading possibilities, opening arrangements or access conditions that are actually known.
Site features may include security-related or operational features only when supplied by real business data.
If map preview is shown here, contact details should still remain on the contact page as the main conversion surface.
Phone, email and address must all be present when available and must not be placeholders.
Contact form should be framed for offert, platsförfrågan or allmän fråga depending on the supplied business data.
If opening hours or map are shown, they must support contact and logistics planning rather than distract from the main CTA.
